ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.

text: “Banaŋ naŋ zoro nembaaloŋ nyɛ la maaloo, bonso, Naaŋmen na zo la ba meŋ nembaaloŋ.
translated: The child who does not listen to the advice of elders will end up eating with pigs, that is how I also ended up eating with pigs.

ref: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Nembeɛoŋ da ŋmaagyili O la, ka O da kpɛ zeŋ gbori poɔ, kyɛ ka a noba zaa are a mane noɔreŋ.
translated: The chief of the land said, and he said bring a sheep, that we will sacrifice it to our ancestors.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: Ka a o daana yeli ko o, garebanyɛ! gbaŋgba-soŋ, fo e la neɛ naŋ seŋ ne dɛloo. Fo moɔ la nimiri kaa bonfee zu velaa, N na e la ka fo kaara bon-yaga zu. Kpɛ wa laŋne ma a wono noɔ.
translated: And then the child said to him, poor thing! foolish person, you don't know that your mouth is a very sharp knife. You don't know that every word you speak has an effect, and I will tell you that your words can kill. That's why I'm warning you.

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: A Yeezu naŋ da wa woŋ a o yelyelii baare, O noɔre zaa da maaɛ la ka O leɛ yeli ko a noba naŋ tuuro O, “Yelmeŋɛ ka N yele korɔ yɛ, N daŋ ba nyɛ neɛ naŋ taa a yeldeebo ŋa tɔ a Izerayɛl poɔ.
translated: And it shall be that when the Lord has given you rest from all your enemies around, in the land which the Lord your God is giving you to possess as an inheritance, you shall blot out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven, do not forget.

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Azuiŋ a ba e yɛ Saa naŋ be tenvelaa poɔ poboɔbo ka a bibiiri bama kanzaa na bɔre.” Taaba Wuluu Yɛlɛ
translated: The elders say that a child who does not listen to his or her parents will be consumed by poverty and hunger in the future.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: A Gyɔɔn da su la bontaŋ kɔɔloŋ kparoo kyɛ da seɛ gane kpagre o seɛŋ, a da ɔɔrɔ sobo kyɛ dire tammalaara.
translated: The hunter went to the bush and killed a big animal and brought it home to his wife, and she cooked it and they ate it together.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.

translated: The child and all the elders went to the farm, and when they got there, they found that the child had already harvested the whole farm.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: A ayi soba meŋ waa ŋa a dɛndɛŋ soba, ‘Nɔŋ fo tɔ a seŋ fo meŋɛ.’

translated: And it came to pass as Jesus was praying in a certain place, when he ceased, one of his disciples said unto him, Lord, teach us to pray, as John also taught his disciples. Book of Luke

translated: The chief of all the villages came to our town yesterday and the people were very happy, and the drummers also drummed and the people danced and played

text: De a fo san-yɔɔ gɛrɛ ne; maa la boɔrɔ ka n yɔɔ a puori deme bama a seŋ fo.
translated: If you want to go and farm, then go and clear a farm for me to sow my guinea corn seeds there.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Ka a Yeezu wa seene ba, a yele, “Yɛ iri, yɛ ta zoro dabeɛ̃.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.”

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: ba puoro ma la weɛlɛ, a wullo nensaala begɛ aŋa a naŋ waa Naaŋmen begɛ.’

ref: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
text: A Yeezu da ba toŋ toŋkpeɛne yaga a be, ba naŋ da ba taa yeldeebo zuiŋ.
translated: If you want to go quickly, go alone, but if you want to go far, go together.

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Serɛzaa ka N yele korɔ yɛ, bonna zaa yɛ naŋ leŋ a teŋɛzu kyɛ, ba na leŋ o la a tenvelaa poɔ, kyɛ bonna zaa meŋ yɛ naŋ lori a teŋɛzu, ba na lori o la a tenvelaa poɔ.
translated: If a person has a farm, all the children will go and help the father to farm, when they go and come back, all the children will go and help the mother to cook, when they go and come back

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Ba naŋ da wa woŋ a naa noɔre yɛlɛ baare, ba da yi gɛrɛ la, ka a ŋmarbiri na ba naŋ da nyɛ a sapareŋ la lɛ, a wa de ba weɛ, a gɛrɛ te kyulli are a zie na a bie naŋ be.
translated: If you want to go and get something, you have to prepare yourself, and when you are prepared, you can go and get it, and you will be happy and you will have peace of mind and you will be content.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: A Yeezu da are la a gɔbena niŋesogɔ, ka a gɔbena soore O, “Fõõ la a Gyuumine Naa be?” Ka a Yeezu yeli ko o, “Fõõ la yeli a lɛ.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "What are you looking for?" And Jesus said to them, "What are you looking for?" or more naturally, "Jesus asked them, 'What are you looking for?' And Jesus replied, 'What do you want?'"

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: A naa poɔ da sãã la yaga, kyɛ a noɔre o naŋ da pɔ ane a saama zaa zuiŋ, o da ba boɔrɔ ka o mɔŋ a pɔgsarlee a Gyɔɔn zu.
translated: When the sun rises, all the animals go to their hiding places, and the elephant goes to the Gyɔɔn river to bathe.

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Ka ba naŋ wa taa yɛ gaa ne sɛrediribe zie, yɛ ta wa miine lɛ yɛ naŋ na yeli, bonso, ka a saŋ wa ta, ba na wuli yɛ la lɛ yɛ naŋ na yeli;
translated: When we go to the farm to sow, we take our food along and when we are there, we eat, and then return home, and when we are back, we eat again

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: A lɛ la ka a Faraziimine ane a Naaŋmen bege wulwulibo da soore a Yeezu, “Bonso ka a fo potuuribo ba tuuro a sããkommine yeltuuri kyɛ dire ne nudɛgɛ?”
translated: And he said to his disciples, “Why do you call me Lord, Lord, and do not do the things I say?

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Kyɛ ba naŋ da wa nyɛ O ka O kyɛnɛ a koɔ̃ zu, ba da teɛre ka nenseɛ la, ka ba da ŋmeɛrɛ kpɛlle yaga zaa;
translated: When they came to the other side, to the country of the Gerasenes, as soon as He stepped out of the boat, a man from the city who had demons met Him, and for a long time he had worn no clothes, and he did not live in a house but in the tombs.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: A noba naŋ da waa yaga a fogle a zie zaa, ka ba kontoɔ̃ taa a baala ta ne a Yeezu zie zuiŋ, ba da do la a peɛzu a te ŋmaa sulee, a de a dɔɔ ne a boŋganaa turi a sulee noɔre ka o sigi te ta benee a Yeezu naŋ zeŋ a die poɔ.
translated: And Jesus went to the other side of the sea of Galilee, and a great crowd followed him because they saw the signs which he had performed on those who were diseased, and they went up into the hills and Jesus went up there and sat down with his disciples, and the passover, the festival of the Jews, was near.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: A beri na poɔ, Yeezu da yi la Naazɛrɛ naŋ be Galili paaloŋ a wa ka Gyɔɔn ko O suobu a Gyɔɔdan baa poɔ.
translated: And Jesus being full of the Holy Spirit returned from Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness
